$comment: c158 roadmap C2 (D-LK-IMAGE-DATA-SLOT-EXTERN-ADDR, ELF/Mach-O positive witness): `FILE **pp = &stdout;` -- the address of libc's extern FILE* stream object in a file-scope initializer -- is LEGAL and satisfies cross-image pointer identity (C11 6.5.9). ELF exec binds via COPY RELOCATION (the .bss copy slot IS the object, so &stdout is a genuine link-time address); Mach-O exec (stdio macro rewrites stdout->__stdoutp) emits a SYMBOL-BASED dyld BIND (c153 fold: slot zeroed on disk, dyld writes the resolved &__stdoutp). Both make pp == &stdout hold -> exit 42; the extra *pp != stdout check proves the slot dereferences to the LIVE stream. The optimized arms prove the address materialization survives the release pipelines. This is the runtime counterpart to the fail-loud PE sibling extern_data_addr_reject_pe (PE has no symbol-based image reloc). ELF x86_64 witnessed locally under WSL (exit 42); the elf-arm64 (qemu) + macho-arm64 arms run on the linux/macos CI legs (SkippedCrossHost compile-clean elsewhere). Mach-O's symbol-based bind is additionally decoded structurally, host-independently, by MachoExecSourceExternSlot.StdoutAddrStaticInitEmitsSymbolBasedBindThroughShippedStdio. RED-ON-DISABLE: bake the indirection-slot VA instead of the symbol-based reloc -> pp is one indirection off -> pp != &stdout -> exit 1.
